task.title: Send an API Request
task.shortdesc: Configure and send an HTTP request in Postman to test an API endpoint and inspect the response.

prereq.p: Before you send an API request, ensure that you have created a collection to save the request to. For instructions, see Create a Collection.

context.p: Sending a request in Postman involves selecting the HTTP method, entering the endpoint URL, configuring parameters or a request body, and selecting Send. Postman displays the response in the response viewer below the request builder.

step.cmd: Select New in the top navigation bar and select HTTP, or select + to open a new request tab.
In the method selector on the left of the URL bar, select the HTTP method for the request: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, or DELETE.
In the URL bar, type the full endpoint URL.
To add query parameters, select the Params tab and enter key-value pairs in the table.
To add an authorization header, select the Authorization tab, select the authorization type from the Auth Type dropdown, and enter the required credentials.
For POST, PUT, or PATCH requests, select the Body tab and configure the request body.

substep.cmd: Select raw and choose JSON from the format dropdown to send a JSON body.
Type or paste the JSON request body in the text area.

stepxmp.p: Example JSON request body for creating a user:

step.stepresult: Postman sends the request and displays the response in the response viewer. The status code, response time, and response size appear in the header of the response panel.

step.cmd: Review the response in the Body tab of the response viewer.
To save the request to a collection, select Save, choose a collection and folder, and select Save.

r.p: Postman sends the request and displays the response. The status code appears in the upper-right of the response panel. For a list of common status codes and their meanings, see Status Codes.

postreq.p: Add test scripts to validate the response automatically. For instructions, see Add Test Scripts.
